evidence_excerpt: At age 7 years, the patient (Family 2, II-3) was alive with persisting hypotonia, global developmental delay, dysarthria, and clumsy/ataxic gait; no sustained improvement or documented deterioration was reported.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing multi-system symptoms: global developmental delay, hypotonia, dysarthria, ataxia, esotropia, and feeding difficulties persisted through childhood.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Secundum atrial septal defect (ASD) diagnosed by echocardiogram.

notes_on_uncertainty: The article reports multiple affected individuals with PPP1R21 loss-of-function. This summary focuses on Family 2, individual II-3, who has the clearest long-term follow-up (age 7 years, alive). Precise diagnosis timing is not provided, so event timing relative to index is marked unknown.
